calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-10-2025 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion To File Confidential Records Under Seal; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Oct-08-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT YAMAHA CORPORATION OF AMERICA'S Motion To File Confidential Records Under Seal

The court exercises its discretion to hear the unopposed motion to seal on shortened time and grants the motion, ordering sealed the unredacted declaration of Kris Paquin. The court finds that Yamaha's disclosure of sales information would result in competitive injury. The court finds that (1) There exists an overriding interest that overcomes the right of public access to the record; (2) The overriding interest supports sealing the record; (3) A substantial probability exists that the overriding interest will be prejudiced if the record is not sealed; (4) The proposed sealing is narrowly tailored; and (5) No less restrictive means exist to achieve the overriding interest.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Court Approval And Entry Of Consent Judgments As To Yamaha Corporation Of America And Khs America, Inc.
Off Calendar Oct-08-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF CENTER FOR ENVIRONMENTAL HEALTH, A NON-PROFIT CORPORATION'S Motion For Court Approval And Entry Of Consent Judgments As To Yamaha Corporation Of America And Khs America, Inc.

The court grants the unopposed motion to approve the Proposition 65 settlement and consent judgment. The court finds that the warning required by the settlement is in compliance with Proposition 65 (Health & Safety Code sections 25249.5 to 25249.14); that the award of attorney's fees pursuant to the settlement is reasonable under California law; and that, considering the factors set out in Health & Safety Code section 25249.7, subdivision (b)(2), and the facts asserted in the moving papers, the penalty amount is reasonable. Accordingly the motion is granted and the parties may submit the proposed judgment for signature.

Proposed order and consent judgment submitted.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Court Approval And Entry Of Consent Judgments As To Yamaha Corporation Of America And Khs America, Inc.
Off Calendar Sep-17-2025 Continued To Oct-08-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 301
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances
Patrick Carey, Esq., LEXINGTON LAW GROUP, LLP (415) 913-7800 pcarey@lexlawgroup.com Attorney for Plaintiff CENTER FOR ENVIRONMENTAL HEALTH
DAVID P. ADAMS, Esq., BLAXTER | BLACKMAN LLP (415) 500-7700 dadams@blaxterlaw.com Attorney for Defendant KHS AMERICA, INC via Zoom Conference

Hearing is held. Matter is discussed. Not argued. Plaintiff's counsel seeks clarification of the Court's request for additional information. Clarification provided.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF CENTER FOR ENVIRONMENTAL HEALTH's Motion For Court Approval And Entry Of Consent Judgments As To Yamaha Corporation Of America And Khs America, Inc.

The court continues the matter for further hearing to October 8, 2025. The motion provides insufficient information from which the court may assess the reasonableness of the proposed civil penalty payments because it lacks information about the number of products sold by each defendant. That the settling plaintiff received the information and believes it has adequately accounted for the number of products sold does not obviate the need for the court to consider this information rather than accepting a party's conclusions. The parties may supplement no later than five court days in advance of the continued hearing date.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)
